content.full_text:
June 10, 2002

Susan A. Shaheen, Ph.D.
Partners for Advanced Transit & Highways
Institute of Transportation Studies
1357 So. 46th Street, Building 452
Richmond, CA 94804-4698

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-148

Dear Dr. Shaheen:

This letter is in response to your request for clarification of advice provided to you on May 20, 2002, regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1

Specifically, you seek clarification of the following statement: “In a letter dated May 7, 2002, you clarified that you have no intention to compete for research funding from Caltrans or accept private consulting clients.” As you are aware, this was a recitation of the information you provided to the Commission in your memorandum of May 7, 2002. We understand your memorandum to mean that you had no intent to compete as an individual in your private capacity. We did not interpret this to refer to your actions undertaken as an employee of the University of California, Berkeley (“UC-Berkeley”).

You note that your employer, UC-Berkeley, is likely to respond to a Caltrans request for proposal regarding carsharing or smart parking research and that you will be involved in preparing its proposal. As we advised, such conduct would not be prohibited by the Act.

1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kenneth L. Glick
Counsel, Legal Division

KLG: jg
I:\Advice Ltrs/02-148
